Hold-Out Blaster.desc: Small, easily hidden blaster for backup or covert carry. Lower damage and range, but can pass security checks when properly concealed.

Blaster Pistol.desc: Standard civilian blaster pistol. Reliable and widely available across the galaxy. Favored by smugglers and merchants for self-defense.

Ion Pistol.notes: Ionic, +2 damage vs droids/vehicles, Stun setting
Ion Pistol.desc: Specialized sidearm that fires ionized bolts. Less effective against living targets, but excellent for disabling droids, sensors, and light vehicle systems.

BlasTech DL-44.desc: Heavy blaster pistol famously used by Han Solo. Powerful punch in a compact package. Highly sought after; expensive and often restricted.

Blaster Rifle (Standard).desc: Standard military blaster rifle. Good balance of power and accuracy. Used by militias, pirates, and resistance forces across the galaxy.

E-11 Stormtrooper Rifle.desc: Imperial standard-issue blaster rifle. Compact, reliable, with folding stock. Can fire three-round bursts. Common on the black market.

Blaster Carbine.desc: Compact long arm balancing mobility and stopping power. Popular with scouts, pilots, and boarding teams.

Ion Rifle.desc: Long-arm ion weapon built to disable droids and light vehicles. Excellent support weapon against mechanized threats.

Blaster Rifle (Heavy).desc: Heavy assault rifle with three-round burst. Devastating against armored targets. Requires strength to wield effectively; used by heavy weapons specialists.

A280 Blaster Rifle.desc: Alliance favorite; powerful and accurate. Superior to E-11 in most respects. Favored by Rebel infantry and veterans.

Light Repeating Blaster.desc: Portable support blaster designed for sustained fire. Devastating in suppression roles, but heavy and difficult to conceal.

Heavy Repeating Blaster.desc: Tripod- or harness-mounted heavy weapon for battlefield dominance. Used by entrenched defenders and heavy assault teams.

Vibroblade.desc: Vibrating blade that cuts through armor. Silent and deadly for close-quarters combat. Popular among assassins and those who prefer stealth.

Vibro-ax.desc: Heavy vibro-weapon; powerful strikes but harder to parry with. Common among Wookiees and other strong species.

Combat Knife.desc: Simple metal blade. Cheap, reliable, and always useful. Standard issue for soldiers; essential backup when blasters fail.

Electrostaff.desc: Electrified pole weapon used by MagnaGuards. Can block lightsabers. Extended reach; requires training to use effectively.

Lightsaber.desc: Elegant weapon of a more civilized age. Plasma blade that cuts through nearly anything. Requires kyber crystal and Force attunement; extremely rare and illegal in Imperial space.

Vibrosword.desc: Powered vibroblade with dueling balance and armor-cutting edge. A premium melee weapon for nobles, mercenaries, and elite enforcers.

Force Pike.desc: Polearm favored by ceremonial guards and security forces. Combines armor-penetrating thrusts with controlled stun discharge.

Shock Gloves.desc: Electrified combat gloves for close-quarters takedowns. Favored by enforcers and bounty hunters who need prisoners alive.

Stun Baton.desc: Security-grade impact baton with a shock emitter. Designed for riot control and law enforcement engagements.

No Armor.desc: No protective gear. Maximum mobility, zero protection. Risky but common for those relying on speed or avoiding notice.

Padded Clothing.desc: Light padding beneath clothes. Minimal protection, easily concealed. Common for diplomats and those who need to look unarmed.

Leather/Jacket.desc: Thick leather or reinforced jacket. Slight protection with everyday appearance. Popular with pilots and smugglers.

Combat Armor (Light).desc: Light tactical armor. Good protection without limiting mobility. Standard for mercenaries and security personnel.

Stormtrooper Armor.desc: Imperial stormtrooper armor. White plastoid plate offers solid protection. Imposes Stealth penalty; highly recognizable and illegal for civilians.

Heavy Battle Armor.desc: Full military armor. Excellent protection but heavy and restrictive. Used by assault troops and heavy weapons specialists.

Mandalorian Armor.desc: Beskar or similar alloys. Legendary armor worn by Mandalorian warriors. Resists conventional weapons; extremely rare and valuable.

Comlink.desc: Personal communicator for voice contact. Short range; essential for coordination. Every spacer and soldier carries one.

Macrobinoculars.desc: High-magnification optical device. Extends vision dramatically. Essential for scouts, snipers, and anyone who needs to spot distant targets.

Glow Rod.desc: Portable light source. Illuminates dark corridors, caves, and ship interiors. Standard equipment for exploration.

Medpac.desc: Field medical kit with stimulants, bacta patches, and basic instruments. Grants +2 to Healing rolls; 5 uses before depleted.

Security Kit.desc: Slicing tools, code crackers, and bypass devices. +2 to Electronics or Repair when dealing with locks and security systems.

Tool Kit.desc: General mechanical tools for repairs. +1 to Repair rolls. Every mechanic and starship crew keeps one handy.

Stimpack.desc: Chemical stimulant patch. Removes one level of Fatigue when applied. Quick fix; overuse can be addictive.

Data Pad.desc: Portable computer for data access, maps, and basic slicing. Essential for Use Computer skill. Ubiquitous in the galaxy.

Breath Mask.desc: Filters breathable air in hostile environments. Essential for toxic worlds, space walks, or smoke-filled areas.

Energy Cell (Spare).desc: Replacement power pack for blaster weapons. Lightweight; experienced fighters always carry spares.

Binders.desc: Magnetic or mechanical restraints for prisoners. Standard for bounty hunters, law enforcement, and slavers.

Fusion Cutter.desc: High-temperature cutting tool. Cuts through hull plating, doors, and barriers. Essential for salvage and infiltration.

Grappling Spike Launcher.desc: Launches tethered spike for climbing or crossing gaps. Common among scouts and urban operatives.

Holorecorder.desc: Records and plays holographic messages and imagery. Used for intelligence, messages, and recording evidence.

Long-Range Comlink.desc: Upgraded comlink with boosted transmitter and directional antenna. Supports communication beyond local line of sight when relay networks are available.

Encrypted Comlink Module.desc: Add-on crypto unit for comlinks. Makes slicing or signal interception significantly harder for hostile listeners.

Bacta Patch.notes: Single use, +1 Healing on one treatment
Bacta Patch.desc: Sealed topical bacta strip for emergency field care. Stabilizes patients quickly and improves one immediate Healing attempt.

Antitoxin Injector.desc: Rapid auto-injector loaded with anti-venom compounds. Used by scouts and troopers operating in toxic or venomous environments.

Survival Kit (24h).desc: Compact emergency pack with rations, water purifier, thermal sheet, flare, and distress beacon. Designed to keep one person alive for a day.

Thermal Cloak.desc: Insulated cloak with heat-retaining fibers. Common for operations in polar regions, high-altitude worlds, and unheated ruins.

Slicer Spike.notes: Single use, +1 Electronics on one access attempt
Slicer Spike.desc: Disposable intrusion module used to bypass basic security systems. Favored by slicers and infiltration teams on tight schedules.

Detonator (Breaching Charge).desc: Shaped charge designed to open sealed doors or light barriers with focused force. Standard for boarding and hostage rescue operations.

Scanner Jammer.desc: Portable interference emitter that floods short-range scanners with static and false echoes. Best used for quick evasions or covert movement.

Climbing Gloves (Mag).desc: Magnetic grip gloves with micro-adhesion pads. Improve climbing on hulls, towers, and urban infrastructure.

Tracking Beacon.notes: Attach to target/item; detectable by tuned scanner
Tracking Beacon.desc: Thumb-sized transmitter beacon used to track moving targets, cargo, or vehicles. Popular with bounty hunters and intelligence operatives.
